calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-07-2021 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-12-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Carr for Petitioner.

The Court suggested that the way to proceed should be to admit the Codicil and the Will because the Codicil re-publishes the Will. Mr. Carr stated that he has no problem with that and that his Clients want to get the matter going. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). The will dated February 25, 1994 and the codicial dated June 5, 1997 are admitted. Bond is not required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Clauzel.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Nov-17-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Carr for Petitioner.

The Court expressed it's concerns. Counsel presented oral argument to support his position. The Court would like to see the disclaimers (to be faxed by Counsel). The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Sep-14-2011 Continued To Oct-26-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Carr for Petitioner.

The Petition for Spousal Property was continued to October 26, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further briefing by Counsel which is due by October 12, 2011.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.
